profileStructure.hierarchyDescription: Apple ID → Family Sharing Group → Per-member Apple ID → Screen Time settings + Managed Profiles → Apple TV+ access

technicalRecon.subtitle: Dual-path architecture: iOS Screen Time API (sanctioned) + web player automation (supplemental)

apiArchitecture.title: Apple Dual-Path Architecture
apiArchitecture.description: Apple TV+ integration has two distinct paths. The SANCTIONED path uses Apple's Screen Time API (FamilyControls, ManagedSettings, DeviceActivity frameworks) available to iOS developers with an approved FamilyControls entitlement. This provides device-level app time limits, content restrictions, and usage monitoring. The UNSANCTIONED path uses Playwright browser automation on tv.apple.com for web-specific parental controls. The web player at tv.apple.com has its own independent parental controls system (4-digit passcode + rating restrictions) that does not sync with device-level Screen Time settings.

codeExample.comment: // iOS Screen Time API (sanctioned path) -- ManagedSettings framework
codeExample.exampleComment: // Block the Apple TV app via Screen Time API

endpoints.purpose: Device-level app blocking, time limits, content restrictions, usage monitoring
Web parental controls (4-digit passcode + rating restrictions)
Universal Type System API -- content catalog, metadata, search results
Video playback and FairPlay DRM streaming
endpoints.auth: Apple ID session + FairPlay DRM tokens

endpoints.purpose: Apple ID web authentication for TV app
Apple Media Products API -- content catalog and metadata
Content metadata, ratings, and availability (alternate endpoint)

authDetails.label: Apple ID 2FA
authDetails.description: Every Apple ID requires 2FA. Web login sends a 6-digit code to a trusted device. Cannot be bypassed or automated without user interaction.

authDetails.label: Session Token
authDetails.description: Opaque session token for web player API calls after successful Apple ID login. Tied to browser session.

authDetails.label: FamilyControls Auth
authDetails.description: One-time user authorization on iOS device for Screen Time API access. No credential storage needed. Sanctioned by Apple.

authDetails.label: Web Passcode
authDetails.description: Protects web parental control settings at tv.apple.com. Separate from Screen Time passcode and Apple TV device restrictions passcode.

authDetails.label: FairPlay DRM
authDetails.description: Content delivery protected by Apple's proprietary DRM. Prevents content interception outside Apple ecosystem.

authDetails.label: Device Attestation
authDetails.description: Screen Time and Family Sharing APIs require genuine Apple device attestation. Cannot be replicated by third-party servers.

rateLimitNotes: Screen Time API (iOS) has no rate limiting concerns -- native Apple framework with direct OS access
Web player APIs enforce Apple ID 2FA on every new session/device -- cannot be automated without user interaction
Apple ID security flags unusual login patterns aggressively -- failed auth attempts can lock accounts
Device trust attestation makes server-side API impersonation infeasible for device-level controls
Screen Time settings sync via encrypted iCloud -- not interceptable or readable by third parties
Web session tokens (media-user-token) are short-lived and tied to the authenticated browser session
technicalRecon.harCaptureCount: N/A -- no HAR capture performed; research based on documentation analysis and web searches

tosWarning.title: Apple Media Services Terms -- Automated Access
tosWarning.description: Apple's Terms of Service strictly prohibit automated access: 'You may not use any software, device, automated process, or any similar or equivalent manual process to scrape, copy, or perform measurement, analysis, or monitoring of, any portion of the Content or Services.' Additionally: 'You may access our Services only using Apple's software.' The iOS Screen Time API path is ToS-compliant when used with an approved FamilyControls entitlement. Web automation of tv.apple.com violates these terms.

overallRisk.description: Apple ID suspension risk makes web automation dangerous (blast radius: entire Apple ecosystem). iOS Screen Time API is sanctioned and low-risk with proper entitlement.

detectionVectors.mitigation: Cannot be bypassed. Require user to manually provide 2FA code for web sessions. Prefer iOS Screen Time API path.
Minimize re-authentication. Cache web sessions. Use user's own network when possible.
Use genuine iOS device via companion app. Screen Time API requires real device -- no emulation.
Cache media-user-token for as long as valid. Detect expiration and prompt user for re-auth.
Never retry failed auth automatically. Apple locks accounts after suspicious activity. User loses entire Apple ecosystem.
Submit clear, well-documented application emphasizing child safety purpose. Apple has approved similar apps (Canopy, etc.).

mitigationsChecklist: Prioritize iOS Screen Time API path (FamilyControls entitlement) as primary integration
Apply for FamilyControls entitlement early -- Apple review process takes 2-4 weeks
For web automation: require user to manually provide 2FA code (never attempt to automate 2FA)
Cache web sessions as long as possible to minimize re-authentication
Never retry failed Apple ID authentication automatically -- account lockout risk
Build iOS companion app with SwiftUI for FamilyControls authorization flow
Clearly communicate to parents that Apple ID credentials are high-sensitivity
Maintain device-level enforcement as primary strategy; web automation as supplemental only
Monitor Apple Developer News for Screen Time API updates and new capabilities

stats.label: Phosra-Added

gapOpportunities.title: Viewing History Visibility
gapOpportunities.gap: Apple TV+ provides no viewing history accessible to parents. Only a 'Continue Watching' row exists with no timestamps, no duration data, and no historical log. Screen Time shows aggregate app usage time only, not specific content watched.
gapOpportunities.solution: Phosra's iOS companion app uses DeviceActivity to track Apple TV app usage time. For web users, Phosra scrapes Continue Watching titles (limited data). Both are surfaced in the Phosra parental dashboard alongside data from other platforms.

gapOpportunities.title: Activity Alerts
gapOpportunities.gap: Apple sends no real-time viewing alerts to parents. Screen Time weekly reports provide aggregate usage but no per-title viewing notifications or content-specific alerts.
gapOpportunities.solution: Phosra's iOS companion app uses DeviceActivity threshold alerts to notify parents when the child approaches or exceeds Apple TV app time limits. Phosra centralizes these alerts with notifications from other platforms.

gapOpportunities.title: Cross-Platform Content Policy
gapOpportunities.gap: Apple TV+ has the most fragmented parental control landscape of any platform: web controls, tvOS device controls, and iOS Screen Time are three independent systems that do not sync. A child restricted on the web can watch unrestricted on an iPad if Screen Time is not configured.
gapOpportunities.solution: Phosra provides a single dashboard where parents define content policies once. Phosra translates the policy into specific instructions for each Apple control surface (web, Screen Time, tvOS) and uses the iOS companion app to enforce device-level restrictions automatically via ManagedSettings.

credentials.name: Child Apple ID mapping
credentials.purpose: Map Phosra child to Apple ID

integrationGap.apiDescription: Screen Time API (iOS, sanctioned) + Playwright (web, supplemental)

steps.title: Authorize
steps.detail: Parent installs Phosra iOS companion app and grants FamilyControls authorization. One-time setup. Phosra gains sanctioned access to set app limits, content restrictions, and monitor usage on the child's device.

steps.title: Configure
steps.description: Set content policy and time limits
steps.detail: Parent defines Apple TV+ policy in Phosra (rating tier, daily time limit, bedtime schedule). Phosra translates to ManagedSettings: sets content restriction level, App Limit for Apple TV app, and Downtime schedule.

steps.title: Enforce
steps.detail: Screen Time enforces the policy at the OS level. When daily time limit is reached, Apple TV app is blocked. During Downtime hours, Apple TV app is inaccessible. Content above the set rating tier is hidden.

steps.title: Notify
steps.detail: Phosra's iOS companion app receives DeviceActivity callbacks when time thresholds are approached or reached. Parent receives push notification via Phosra app with usage summary.

limitations.title: Requires Apple devices
limitations.description: Screen Time API integration requires iOS/tvOS devices. Families accessing Apple TV+ via web (PC, Chromebook, Android) cannot use the sanctioned path and must rely on web automation or manual setup.

limitations.title: No content-level visibility
limitations.description: Screen Time reports total Apple TV app usage time but not specific titles watched. Phosra cannot tell parents WHAT the child watched, only HOW LONG they used the Apple TV app.

limitations.title: No per-title blocking
limitations.description: Apple TV+ does not support blocking specific titles. Restrictions are rating-based only. A parent cannot block one specific TV-14 show while allowing others at the same rating.

enforcementFlow.loopLabel: Repeats daily until parent adjusts limits

methods.name: authenticate
methods.recommendation: iOS: FamilyControls one-time authorization. Web: Playwright login with user-provided 2FA code. Strongly prefer iOS path.

methods.name: listProfiles
methods.recommendation: Web player is single-user per Apple ID. Map children to Family Sharing Apple IDs, not profiles. Limited utility.

methods.name: getProfileSettings
methods.recommendation: iOS: Read restriction state via ManagedSettings. Web: Scrape tv.apple.com settings page (requires passcode entry).

methods.name: setContentRestrictions
methods.recommendation: iOS: Direct ManagedSettings API call (sanctioned). Web: Playwright with passcode entry. iOS path is preferred.

methods.name: setTitleRestrictions
methods.recommendation: Apple TV+ does not support per-title blocking. Return UnsupportedOperationError.

methods.name: setProfilePIN
methods.recommendation: Set/change the 4-digit web parental controls passcode via Playwright. Note: separate from Screen Time passcode and tvOS restrictions passcode.

methods.name: getWatchHistory
methods.recommendation: Very sparse data. Web: scrape Continue Watching titles only. iOS: DeviceActivity provides app usage time (no titles). Set expectations accordingly.

methods.name: lockProfile
methods.recommendation: iOS: Block Apple TV app via ManagedSettings (sanctioned, immediate). Web: Set restrictions to 'Don't Allow' as blunt lock mechanism.

methods.name: createKidsProfile
methods.recommendation: Do not automate. Guide parents through manual child Apple ID creation or managed profile setup. Involves identity verification and legal consent steps.

methods.name: supportsNativeScreenTime
methods.recommendation: Returns TRUE (conditionally). Apple Screen Time provides native daily limits, Downtime scheduling, App Limits, and weekly usage reports. Best native screen time of any streaming platform -- but device-level only.

timelinePhases.label: iOS Foundation
timelinePhases.components: FamilyControls entitlement (2-4 weeks Apple review), iOS companion app scaffold (5-7d)
